description:
Hi team,

Frank Sutton at Tri-State Logistics here. We're sourcing axle assemblies from a Mexican supplier and want to confirm whether they qualify for USMCA preferential treatment. The supplier sent a certification but I'm not sure the regional value content math holds. Can you review the RVC calculation before we claim origin on the next entry?

Thanks,
Frank
subject: USMCA rule-of-origin question on imported axle assemblies

description:
Hi team,

Wendell Pierce at Tri-State Logistics here. Need to confirm the HTS code for refrigerated trailer evaporator units. We've been entering them under 8418 but a broker at another lane used 8708. Which is correct for a unit shipped separately from the trailer?

Thanks,
Wendell
subject: HTS classification confirmation for refrigerated trailer parts

description:
Hi team,

Kevin Tran at Tri-State Logistics here. Our import volume is up roughly 30 percent this year. Is our current continuous bond still sufficient, or do we need to increase the bond amount before CBP flags it?

Thanks,
Kevin
subject: Customs bond sufficiency for higher import volume

description:
Hi team,

Samuel Ortega at Tri-State Logistics here. A container's ISF went in two days after the carrier cutoff because of a vendor delay. What penalty exposure are we looking at and how should we document the reasonable care?

Thanks,
Samuel
subject: ISF late filing — what's our exposure

description:
Hi team,

Lorraine Diaz at Tri-State Logistics here. We re-exported a batch of components that were originally imported with duty paid. Do these qualify for unused-merchandise drawback, and what records do we need to keep for the claim?

Thanks,
Lorraine
subject: Duty drawback eligibility on re-exported goods

description:
Hi team,

Bernard Holt at Tri-State Logistics here. Two SKUs we import from China are showing a Section 301 additional duty. Is there an exclusion in effect for these, and can we request one if not?

Thanks,
Bernard
subject: Section 301 tariff applicability on Chinese-origin parts

description:
Hi team,

Camille Royce at Tri-State Logistics here. We repackage imported product into retail cartons here in the US. Does the origin marking need to appear on the retail carton, the individual unit, or both?

Thanks,
Camille
subject: Country-of-origin marking requirements for repackaged goods

description:
Hi team,

Michael Donnelly at Maumee Valley Foods here. Setting up FDA prior notice for a new canned vegetable line. Which fields are mandatory for the prior notice confirmation and who should be listed as the US agent?

Thanks,
Michael
subject: FDA prior notice fields for canned vegetable import

description:
Hi team,

Karen Whitfield at Maumee Valley Foods here. We flagged several entries for reconciliation on value. What's the deadline to file the recon entry and can it be extended if our transfer-pricing study isn't final?

Thanks,
Karen
subject: Reconciliation entry deadline question

description:
Hi team,

Gerald Maddox at Maumee Valley Foods here. Received a notice that a steel fastener import may fall under an AD order. Can you check the scope ruling and tell us if our product is covered before the next shipment lands?

Thanks,
Gerald
subject: Antidumping duty scope on steel fasteners

description:
Hi team,

Rachel Combs at Maumee Valley Foods here. We want certainty on the classification of a new electronic sensor assembly before we commit to a supply contract. Can you prepare a binding ruling request to CBP on our behalf?

Thanks,
Rachel
subject: Binding ruling request for a new sensor assembly

description:
Hi team,

Diane Foster at Maumee Valley Foods here. We're weighing an FTZ subzone against a bonded warehouse for our cold-storage operation. Which gives us better duty deferral for goods that may be re-exported?

Thanks,
Diane
subject: Foreign-trade zone vs bonded warehouse for cold storage

description:
Hi team,

Paul Kessler at Maumee Valley Foods here. We paid for tooling at our overseas supplier. Do those tooling costs get added to the customs value as an assist, and how do we apportion them across shipments?

Thanks,
Paul
subject: Valuation question — assists and tooling costs

description:
Hi team,

Yolanda Pace at Maumee Valley Foods here. We found an entry from last month was classified under the wrong HTS. Should we file a PSC to correct the duty, and is there interest owed on the difference?

Thanks,
Yolanda
subject: Post-summary correction for misclassified entry

description:
Hi team,

Frank Sutton at Tri-State Logistics here. Our power of attorney with your firm is coming up on its review date. Can you send the renewal so there's no gap in your authority to file our entries?

Thanks,
Frank
subject: Broker power of attorney renewal

description:
Hi team,

Wendell Pierce at Tri-State Logistics here. We import small product samples for our quality lab. Do these qualify for de minimis entry, and is there a value threshold per shipment we need to stay under?

Thanks,
Wendell
subject: De minimis treatment for low-value samples

description:
Hi team,

Kevin Tran at Tri-State Logistics here. If we change the fiber blend of an imported fabric, can we move it to a lower-duty HTS subheading? Want to make sure any change is legitimate and not just on paper.

Thanks,
Kevin
subject: Tariff engineering question on textile blends

description:
Hi team,

Samuel Ortega at Tri-State Logistics here. We got a CBP Form 28 asking for support on a recent entry's declared value. Can you help us assemble the response and the supporting commercial documents?

Thanks,
Samuel
subject: CBP Form 28 request for information response

description:
Hi team,

Lorraine Diaz at Tri-State Logistics here. How long are we required to keep entry documents and supporting records after the date of entry? Want to set our retention policy correctly.

Thanks,
Lorraine
subject: Recordkeeping retention period for entry documents

description:
Hi team,

Bernard Holt at Tri-State Logistics here. Onboarding a new Canadian grain supplier. What do we need from them to support a USMCA origin claim, and can you give us a template certification of origin?

Thanks,
Bernard
subject: USMCA certification for a new agricultural supplier

description:
Hi team,

Camille Royce at Tri-State Logistics here. We'd like your firm to run a quarterly audit of our entry summaries to catch classification or valuation errors before CBP does. Can we set that up as a standing engagement?

Thanks,
Camille
subject: Quarterly entry summary audit request

description:
Hi team,

Michael Donnelly at Maumee Valley Foods here. Our Section 232 product exclusions are set to lapse. Can you handle the renewal filings so we don't pick up the additional steel duties on the next entries?

Thanks,
Michael
subject: Steel and aluminum 232 exclusion renewal
